title: centaurea biebersteinii subsp. australis
intro: Centaurea biebersteinii subsp. australis, also known as Australian knapweed or Centaurea australis, is a plant species belonging to the asteraceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and coarse leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea biebersteinii subsp. australis include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use through infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, although its use as a medicinal herb should be approached with caution due to potential allergenic effects and lack of comprehensive scientific research.
